summary: Streptomycin remains suitable despite risks because no other antibiotic offers both necessary efficacy without posing unacceptable risk from possible severe allergies; however, careful monitoring will still be essential given inherent toxicities associated with this medication choice compared directly versus more modern options available today which might carry less side effects but insufficient power alone when dealing specifically with certain bacterial infections implicated here such as those causing brucellosis among others listed above by virtue their gram negative nature
